message:
perf(http): fuse colon search and name validation into single-pass header parse

parse_header_line_bounds previously made two passes over the header name
bytes: first scanning for the colon position, then validating each byte
against the token-char table. This commit fuses both operations into a
single forward scan: each byte is checked against is_valid_header_name_byte
while simultaneously searching for the colon delimiter.

The fused loop sets an `invalid_name` flag if any non-token byte is seen
before the colon, deferring the error until after colon discovery so that
missing-colon errors (BadHeader) still take priority over name validation
errors (InvalidHeaderName). The empty-name check simplifies to
`colon == 0`.

Value validation (CR/LF rejection) similarly switches from a functional
`.any()` iterator to an explicit for-loop with early return, which is
more readable and avoids the closure overhead on this hot path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
